title: Forelimb Reaching Task in Mice
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"dabc6","text":"This protocol describes a forelimb reaching task used to study motor learning and memory in mice. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"ajegq","text":"Mice are placed in a reverse 12h light/dark cycle room for at least 1 week prior to training. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e6ii7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fnnhq","text":"Food restriction (2 days):","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":26}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"71prd","text":"●      Mice are food restricted and fed with ~0.1 grams of food per 1 gram of body weight per day. After 2 days, their body weight should be reduced to 85-90% of baseline weight and maintained throughout training.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"20hks","text":"●      If TRAP mice are used and 4-Hydroxytamoxifen (4-OHT) injections are planned during training, mice      are habituated to the injection procedure. Starting on the same day as food restriction, mice receive 0.3 ml saline injections IP daily throughout the experiments. Food chow is provided as a reward immediately after injections.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"95usl","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"62bo8","text":"Wear appropriate PPE as required by your institution. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"1rc32","text":"The behavior chamber is a transparent Plexiglas box (20cm height, 15cm depth, 8.5cm width) with a vertical slit opening on the front (0.5cm width) (Figure 1/image rendering of behavior chamber is associated with this protocol).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Shaping Day 1 -One or two mice (same sex and previously housed together) are placed inthe behavior chamber for 20 mins with food pellets provided on the chamberfloor.
Shaping Day 2 -Mice are placed individually in the chamber with food pellets provided on the chamber floor.
Shaping Days 3~7 -A tray filled with food pellets is placed against the wall outside the chamber, allowing the mice to reach the pellets through the chamber opening.

Mice are placed individually in the chamber for 20 minutes or until they made 5 consecutive reaching attempts using one limb or a total of 20 reaching attempts. The goal of shaping on Day 3 is to determine the dominant limb of each mouse. Shaping is considered complete when a mouse makes 5 consecutive reaching attempts using one limb or made over 70% of its attempts using the same limb (dominant limb).
Note: Although training typically begins the day after shaping, it is acceptable to wait a few daysbetween shaping and training.
Shaped mice are trained for 8-10 days. Each day, mice are placed individually in the chamber and trained to reach, grasp, and retrieve food pellets from a platform using their dominant limbs.
This process could be repeated for up to 5 days (total of 7 days). Mice not meeting the criteria after 5 days are dropped from the experiment.
Mice are trained for 30 reaches or 20 minutes per day, whichever came first.

Criteria for reach performance is monitored by recording the type of each reach: ● Success: The mouse reaches with its preferred limb, grasps the pellet, retrieves it, and feeds it into its mouth. ● Drop: The mouse reaches with its preferred limb, grasps the pellet, but drops it before feeding. ● Fail: The mouse reaches with its preferred limb but misses or knocks the pellet off the holding plate. ● In-vain Reaches: Reaches made when no pellet is on the platform; not counted towards total reaching attempts. ● Contralateral Reaches: Reaches made with the non-dominant limb; not counted towards total reaching attempts. Success rate is calculated by the formula: Number of Success reaches / (Success + Drop + Fail reaches). In-vain and contralateral reaches are not included.
Early TRAPed Group: Mice received 4-OHT injections on Training Day 1 and 2. Training is stopped after Day 2.
Mice are trained for 30 reaches or 20 minutes per day, whichever came first.

Criteria for reach performance is monitored by recording the type of each reach: ● Success: The mouse reaches with its preferred limb, grasps the pellet, retrieves it, and feeds it into its mouth. ● Drop: The mouse reaches with its preferred limb, grasps the pellet, but drops it before feeding. ● Fail: The mouse reaches with its preferred limb but misses or knocks the pellet off the holding plate. ● In-vain Reaches: Reaches made when no pellet is on the platform; not counted towards total reaching attempts. ● Contralateral Reaches: Reaches made with the non-dominant limb; not counted towards total reaching attempts. Success rate is calculated by the formula: Number of Success reaches / (Success + Drop + Fail reaches). In-vain and contralateral reaches are not included.
Early TRAPed Continuous Group: Same as Early TRAPed Group but training is continued after Day 2.
Mice are trained for 30 reaches or 20 minutes per day, whichever came first.

Criteria for reach performance is monitored by recording the type of each reach: ● Success: The mouse reaches with its preferred limb, grasps the pellet, retrieves it, and feeds it into its mouth. ● Drop: The mouse reaches with its preferred limb, grasps the pellet, but drops it before feeding. ● Fail: The mouse reaches with its preferred limb but misses or knocks the pellet off the holding plate. ● In-vain Reaches: Reaches made when no pellet is on the platform; not counted towards total reaching attempts. ● Contralateral Reaches: Reaches made with the non-dominant limb; not counted towards total reaching attempts. Success rate is calculated by the formula: Number of Success reaches / (Success + Drop + Fail reaches). In-vain and contralateral reaches are not included.
Late TRAPed Group: Mice received 4-OHT injections on Training Day 7 and 8.
Mice are trained for 30 reaches or 20 minutes per day, whichever came first.

Criteria for reach performance is monitored by recording the type of each reach: ● Success: The mouse reaches with its preferred limb, grasps the pellet, retrieves it, and feeds it into its mouth. ● Drop: The mouse reaches with its preferred limb, grasps the pellet, but drops it before feeding. ● Fail: The mouse reaches with its preferred limb but misses or knocks the pellet off the holding plate. ● In-vain Reaches: Reaches made when no pellet is on the platform; not counted towards total reaching attempts. ● Contralateral Reaches: Reaches made with the non-dominant limb; not counted towards total reaching attempts. Success rate is calculated by the formula: Number of Success reaches / (Success + Drop + Fail reaches). In-vain and contralateral reaches are not included.
Control: Mice are exposed to the same environment but not trained to reach food pellets, also received 4-OHT injections.
creator: Christine Weber-Schmidt
